lines: Corin holds the blue ball against his chest with both arms.
"Eda does not kick it inside again. Outside only, once outside comes back."
Corin sees the blue in your pack before you speak.
"You found it. It still belongs to me, right?"

questUpdate.log: Quest complete: Corin has his blue ball again.

effects.log: Hanne Rovik gives you a clean field dressing for bringing the ball back.

lines: Corin points down the central hall, then pulls his hand back fast.
"The ball went past the old shelves; Selka said nobody little goes there, and nobody big should either."

choices.label: Ask what he heard down there

lines: Corin is small enough to vanish behind Selka, but he steps out when he sees you looking at the lower hall.
"Eda kicked my blue ball. She said she could make it hit the wall and come back. It did not come back."
"Down there. We are not allowed deeper, but you are allowed, maybe."

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Corin's blue ball deeper in the catacombs.

choices.label: Ask what he heard down there

lines: "Blue is a proper color."
"Most things here are grey, brown, or the color Hanne makes when she sees a dirty bandage. Blue is better."

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Corin's blue ball deeper in the catacombs.

lines: "Eda says the wall moved. I said walls do not move unless the Host is in them. She said not that kind of moved."
"She is sorry. I can tell because she keeps standing where I can see her being sorry."
"After one day. Maybe two if she looks smug."
"Forgiving too fast teaches bad kicking."
"Outside has sky and shouting. Down here has whispers and grown people pretending whispers are pipes."
"I like sky better, even when it rains ash. At least ash is honest about landing on you."
"After the ball stopped, something tapped three times."
"I tapped back once. Selka heard and used my whole name, so I stopped being brave."
"It did not feel like a choice. It felt like Selka."
